basic_info.name: Balian (Chinese: 巴里安; Indonesian: Pantai Balian / Suraberata)
basic_info.gps_location: Approx. -8.5917, 114.9700 (Pantai Balian, Lalanglinggah, Selemadeg Barat, Tabanan Regency, Bali 82162; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Balian) [uncertain]
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~70-75 km (approx. 1.5-2 hours drive depending on traffic)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-100 min by motorbike (~50 km); From Uluwatu: ~120-150 min by motorbike (~80 km)

surf_conditions.wave_type: River-mouth break with both left and right peaks over cobblestone and sand bottom; the left typically runs longer and more reliable while the right is steeper and can pitch into a barrel; an additional beach-break section forms south of the rivermouth at low tide
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate primary; accessible to advanced beginners on smaller days; advanced surfers find punchy barrel sections on bigger swells
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-8 ft; works well at 2-6 ft (head-high range); manageable up to overhead+ for advanced surfers
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Cobblestone with sand deposits from the river mouth; not sharp reef - more forgiving than Bukit reefs
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season April-October (peak May-September); fairly consistent year-round, often working when south Bukit is flat; avoid first storms of wet season due to pollution
surf_conditions.best_tide: Works on all tides; mid tide considered best for organised peaks; low tide produces beach-break section at the river mouth; high tide softens the wave
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore N to NE; early morning land breeze ideal; SE trade winds cross-shore by mid-morning
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SW (similar window to Medewi and Bukit); responsive to most Indian Ocean groundswells
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (one of the most consistent breaks in Bali - 'ultra consistent' year-round; rideable when much of the island goes flat)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 50-150 meters typical (lefts run longer than rights; multiple connecting sections on the best sandbank days) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium (more punch than Medewi; lefts can wall up fast on bigger days; rights pitch quickly into barrel sections)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Low to moderate - friendly cobble/sand bottom (not razor reef), but shifting peaks require positioning; rivermouth current can pull surfers off the peak
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - distinct left peak, right peak, and beach-break section at the rivermouth south of the main peak; channel between left and right makes paddle-out easy
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 2-3 (generally low; busier on weekends and during epic swells with traveling surfers and small surf-camp groups; far less crowded than Canggu/Bukit)

practical_info.access: Drive west on Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk; turn south at Lalanglinggah/Suraberata toward Pantai Balian; park at the cliff-top area or beachfront warungs/guesthouses; walk down the path/stairs to the beach
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Easy paddle-out through the channel between the left and right peaks (deep water between them); for the rivermouth section, paddle out along the southern beach edge
practical_info.entry_fee: No formal beach entry fee; parking IDR 5,000-10,000 motorbike / IDR 10,000-20,000 car at beachfront warungs
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for offshore winds and uncrowded peaks; late afternoon glass-off as winds soften
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~9 AM; second window late afternoon; avoid mid-day when SE trade winds disrupt the surface
practical_info.nearby_warung: Pondok Pitaya Beach Restaurant, Sushi Surf Balian, Deki's Warung, Tekor Bali, Balian Beach Cafe, Made's Homestay warung - all clustered around the beachfront and clifftop area
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Limited within 10 min - Balian itself has multiple peaks (left, right, rivermouth beach break); Soka Beach and Kelating ~20-30 min east for backup beach-break options
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - direct beach access via short walk from parking

safety_culture.hazards: Strong rivermouth current especially after rain, occasional bull shark sightings (historic attacks reported), pollution from stormwater runoff after rain, isolated bigger-day rip currents, slippery cobblestones at entry/exit
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Occasional bull sharks reported off the coast (rare attacks over the past decade especially in murky water at dawn/dusk near the rivermouth); sea urchins on rocky sections; occasional jellyfish; rare sea snake sightings
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Low - cobblestone and sand bottom not razor reef; some cuts possible from barnacle-covered rocks but generally one of the more forgiving Bali bottoms
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Rivermouth current drains south and offshore - useful as paddle-out lane but strong after rain; channel between left and right peaks acts as natural paddle-out; side current can pull surfers toward the rivermouth
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Relaxed compared to Bukit but still observe priority; defer to local Indonesians and regulars; do not snake; greet locals; rotation respected on the left point
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low - sleepy village atmosphere; friendly hosts; minimal localism pressure; vibe described as reminiscent of pre-tourism Bali 1970s
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Be friendly, support local warungs and homestays, respect Hindu temple ceremonies in nearby Lalanglinggah, do not paddle out drunk or disrespect locals; observe priority and let regulars get waves
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Local Tabanan surfers based at Pondok Pitaya and Balian Surf Camp; longstanding instructor/guide community at Casa Balian and Balian Spirit [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Hindu Balinese village (unlike Muslim Medewi/Yeh Sumbul); nearby temples include Pura Dang Kahyangan Gading Wani, Luhur Srijong Temple (10 min east) and Pura Tanah Lot region further east; respect temple ceremonies (odalan), modest dress when entering temple grounds, women on menstruation traditionally avoid temples; Nyepi (Day of Silence ~March) - no surfing/beach access islandwide
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Klinik/Puskesmas Selemadeg (basic primary care, ~10-15 min); RSUD Tabanan / Tabanan Hospital (~45-60 min east); BIMC and Siloam Denpasar ~1.5-2 hours east for serious cases
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: ~5-10 km to Puskesmas Selemadeg; ~30-40 km to Tabanan Hospital
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No permanent rescue jet-ski; rely on self-rescue and surf-camp instructors
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Police 110; Ambulance 118/119; Tourist Police +62-361-754599 (Bali) [uncertain on most current numbers]

services.nearest_surf_school: Balian Surf Camp (long-running lessons and board rentals), Pondok Pitaya Surf School (hotel-based, all levels), Casa Balian, Balian Spirit Yoga & Surf, Bali Wave Hunter, Pacha Balian Surf Camp
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - readily available from Balian Surf Camp, Pondok Pitaya, beachfront shops and homestays; ~IDR 80,000-150,000/day for shortboards/soft-tops
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - freelance land photographers shoot from the clifftop and beach especially during dry-season swells; less developed scene than Uluwatu but present [uncertain]
services.photographer_recommendation: Inquire at Pondok Pitaya or Balian Surf Camp; freelance shooters via Instagram; typical session IDR 150,000-400,000 [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Pondok Pitaya Hotel Surfing & Yoga (mid-high, beachfront favorite), Balian Paradise Resort, Gubug Balian Beach Bungalows, Casa Balian, Villa Balian (luxury), Balian Spirit, Made's Homestay, various small homestays in Lalanglinggah
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/balian/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d41 (Surfline cam/report); Pondok Pitaya surfcam at https://pondokpitaya.com/surfcam/; balicams.com/camera/balian
services.drone_allowed: Generally permitted for personal use; respect temple grounds and ceremonies; commercial use requires permit [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Limited dedicated coworking; surf-camp wifi (Pondok Pitaya, Casa Balian) sufficient for remote work; nearest serious coworking ~1.5 hours east in Canggu/Tabanan [uncertain]

pricing.avg_lesson_price: IDR 300,000-500,000 (~USD 20-35) for 1-on-1 hour lesson; group lessons IDR 250,000-400,000 per person; multi-day packages available at Balian Surf Camp and Pondok Pitaya

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season; declines significantly in wet season (Nov-April) due to river stormwater runoff carrying agricultural waste, plastic and sediment - avoid surfing for 24-48 hours after heavy rain, especially near the rivermouth

events_community.annual_competition_window: No regular WSL-level event; occasional local Tabanan/Balian surf contests and longboard events during dry season hosted by local surf camps [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Yoga retreats, full-moon gatherings, surf-camp group dinners at Pondok Pitaya, Balinese Hindu temple ceremonies (odalan) in nearby village, occasional live music nights at Balian Beach Cafe [uncertain]
